message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="luca-palmieri" color="blue" summary="Step 10 implementation ready for team review">
Team, Step 10 (Someday/Maybe, US-GTD-6) is committed locally (e4c7b73). Ready for review.

## Implementation Summary

### Domain: `SomedayMaybeItem` (struct, not enum)
- Simple struct with `id: SomedayMaybeId`, `user_id: UserId`, `title: TodoTitle`, `created_at: DateTime<Utc>`
- No state machine — items are created, edited, activated (moved to inbox), or deleted
- `SomedayMaybeId(Uuid)` newtype with the standard `new()`, `from_uuid()`, `as_uuid()` pattern
- 4 unit tests covering ID uniqueness, field access, from_parts reconstruction

### Database: `someday_maybe_items` table
- Migration `20240101000009`: `id`, `user_id` (FK to users), `title`, `created_at`
- Index on `user_id`

### Repository: `someday_maybe_repository.rs`
- `insert_someday_maybe_item(executor, &item)` — `impl PgExecutor` for transaction support
- `find_someday_maybe_items_by_user`, `find_someday_maybe_item_by_id`, `delete_someday_maybe_item`, `count_someday_maybe_items`, `update_someday_maybe_title`
- Also generalized `inbox_repository::insert_inbox_item` from `&PgPool` to `impl PgExecutor<'_>` (needed for the activate transaction)

### Service: `someday_maybe_service.rs`
- `add_someday_maybe_item` — validates title, persists
- `list_someday_maybe_items` — all items for user, ordered by created_at ASC
- `delete_someday_maybe_item` — ownership check, permanent removal
- `update_someday_maybe_title` — ownership check, title validation, persist
- `activate_someday_maybe_item` — transaction: creates InboxItem with same title, deletes SomedayMaybeItem

### Routes: `routes/someday_maybe.rs` (replaces placeholder)
- `GET /someday-maybe` — list page with add form, items, empty state
- `POST /someday-maybe` — add item (redirect / HTMX fragment)
- `POST /someday-maybe/{id}/activate` — move to inbox (redirect / HTMX empty body + announce "Moved to Inbox")
- `POST /someday-maybe/{id}/delete` — delete item (redirect / HTMX empty body)
- `GET /someday-maybe/{id}/edit` — inline edit form (HTMX)
- `POST /someday-maybe/{id}/edit` — submit edit (redirect / HTMX fragment)
- `GET /someday-maybe/{id}` — single item fragment (for cancel edit)

### Templates
- `someday_maybe.html` — list page with add form, empty state, item loop
- `someday_maybe_item.html` — item with Edit link, Activate button, Delete button
- `someday_maybe_edit.html` — inline edit form with Save button and Cancel link
- All actions have unique accessible `aria-label` including item title

### CSS: BEM `.someday-maybe*` classes in components layer
- Mirrors the waiting-for pattern with simpler structure (no "person" field)
- Uses design tokens throughout (no hardcoded values)

### Tests: 25 new integration tests
- Page loading (4): 200 status, heading, empty state, current page nav
- Add (3): redirect, empty title silently ignored, too-long title 422
- HTMX add (2): fragment + announce, empty title 204
- Activate (3): moves to inbox + redirect, HTMX empty body + announce, nonexistent 404
- Delete (3): redirect, HTMX empty body + announce, nonexistent 404
- Edit (2): redirect, HTMX fragment + announce
- A11y (3): accessible activate, delete, edit labels
- Semantic list (1): `<ul>` with `role="list"`
- User isolation (1): users cannot see each other's items
- Form label (1): visible label "Park an idea"
- HTMX edit form (1): inline form with Save/Cancel
- Single item (1): fragment for cancel edit

### Glossary: Updated with
- `SomedayMaybeItem`, `SomedayMaybeId` types (marked Implemented)
- 4 new actions: add, delete, edit, activate (all marked Implemented)
- 7 error types in Domain Errors table
- 3 error copy entries

### Pipeline: 252 Rust tests (89 unit + 163 integration) + 11 Playwright e2e, all green

Please review and provide your CONSENSUS: APPROVED or blocking feedback.
</teammate-message>

content.text: No focus management JS file for someday-maybe. Let me check if the main template references one, or if it's missing.

The template has no `{% block scripts %}` section, which means there's no focus management JS file. This is a problem -- when items are deleted or activated via HTMX, focus will be lost (sent to `<body>`). Every other list feature (inbox, next actions, projects, waiting for) has a focus management JS file.

input.content:
CONSENSUS: APPROVED with one **BLOCKING** issue.

**BLOCKING: Missing focus management JS.**

The `someday_maybe.html` template has no `{% block scripts %}` section and there is no `static/js/someday-maybe-focus.js` file. Every other list feature has one:
- `static/js/inbox-focus.js`
- `static/js/next-action-focus.js`
- `static/js/waiting-for-focus.js`
- Projects uses `static/js/project-focus.js`

Without focus management JS, when a user activates or deletes a someday/maybe item via HTMX, focus is lost to `<body>`. This violates WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order) and our HTMX Focus Management Protocol from TEAM_AGREEMENTS.md. It also means the empty state toggle won't work (hiding/showing the empty message when the last item is removed or first item is added).

The fix is straightforward -- copy the `waiting-for-focus.js` pattern, adapt the class names to `someday-maybe-item` / `someday-maybe__add` / `someday-maybe-list` / `someday-maybe-empty-state` / `someday-maybe-title`, and add `{% block scripts %}<script src="/static/js/someday-maybe-focus.js"></script>{% endblock %}` to the template.

---

**Everything else: Correct.**

Templates:
- Add form: `hx-post="/someday-maybe"`, `hx-target="#someday-maybe-list"`, `hx-swap="beforeend"` -- correct
- `hx-on::after-request` clears input and refocuses -- correct
- Visible label "Park an idea" -- correct
- Item partial: Edit/Activate/Delete with correct HTMX targeting and aria-labels -- correct
- Edit partial: hx-post with outerHTML swap, cancel via hx-get -- correct
- `<time>` element with ISO datetime -- correct (learned from Step 8)
- `role="list"` on `<ul>` -- correct
- Empty state with `hidden` attribute -- correct

Routes:
- All 7 handlers follow the dual-response pattern correctly
- `htmx_response_with_announce` used for all mutations: "Idea parked", "Moved to Inbox", "Item deleted", "Item updated" -- correct
- Error handling matches established patterns exactly
- `current_page: "someday_maybe"` matches gtd_nav.html -- correct

Route registration:
- All 5 route groups correctly registered in startup.rs -- correct
- mod.rs exports all 7 route handlers -- correct
- Placeholder removed from gtd_placeholders.rs -- correct

**Note on button label**: The activate button says "Activate" with `aria-label="Activate: {{ item.title }}"`. I suggested "Move to Inbox" during design review. The announce text correctly says "Moved to Inbox". Steve Krug should make the final call on the button copy -- this is Minor, not blocking.
input.summary: BLOCKING: missing focus management JS; everything else approved

The `someday_maybe.html` template has no `{% block scripts %}` section and there is no `static/js/someday-maybe-focus.js` file. Every other list feature has one:
- `static/js/inbox-focus.js`

Without focus management JS, when a user activates or deletes a someday/maybe item via HTMX, focus is lost to `<body>`. This violates WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order) and our HTMX Focus Management Protocol from TEAM_AGREEMENTS.md. It also means the empty state toggle won't work (hiding/showing the empty message when the last item is removed or first item is added).

The fix is straightforward -- copy the `waiting-for-focus.js` pattern, adapt the class names to `someday-maybe-item` / `someday-maybe__add` / `someday-maybe-list` / `someday-maybe-empty-state` / `someday-maybe-title`, and add `{% block scripts %}<script src="/static/js/someday-maybe-focus.js"></script>{% endblock %}` to the template.

The announce text correctly says "Moved to Inbox". Steve Krug should make the final call on the button copy -- this is Minor, not blocking.

content.text:
Sent my review with one **BLOCKING** issue: missing `someday-maybe-focus.js` for HTMX focus management after delete/activate operations. Without it, focus is lost to `<body>` when items are removed from the DOM, violating WCAG 2.4.3 and our HTMX Focus Management Protocol. The fix is straightforward -- adapt the waiting-for-focus.js pattern.

Everything else (templates, routes, route registration, HTMX attributes, progressive enhancement) is correct and follows established patterns.
